cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking how long the experiment lasted according to the text. The options are A) about three days, B) about ten days, C) about two weeks, or D) about a month. First, I'll need to look through the text for any mention of the duration of the experiment. The text discusses a study where participants were asked to write for three consecutive days, 20 minutes each day. That's part of the procedure, but the experiment's total duration would include the time before and after the writing sessions. The text says that two weeks after the third day of writing, participants received a skin biopsy. Then, their wounds were photographed regularly over the following days. The healing was monitored, and on the 11th day after the biopsy, they checked the healing status. So, let's break it down. The writing part lasted three days. Then, two weeks after that, they did the biopsy. The monitoring after the biopsy lasted until the 11th day. So the total duration would be the three days of writing, plus two weeks (which is 14 days) until the biopsy, and then 11 days after the biopsy for monitoring. But maybe the question is about the entire experiment from start to finish. Wait, the experiment might have started when the participants began writing. The three days of writing, then two weeks later the biopsy was done. The healing period after the biopsy was 11 days. So from the start of the writing (day 1) to the 11th day after the biopsy would be 3 days (writing) + 14 days (until biopsy) + 11 days (monitoring) = 28 days. That would be about a month, which is option D. However, the question is phrased as "the experiment lasted," so perhaps the main duration is from the start of the writing to the end of the monitoring.
